mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or unemployment spikes fail to predict negative returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high retail sales growth and declining consumer price inflation indicate robust consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansions, or the signal reverses direction in > 50% of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.5 percent year-over-year forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation periods consistently yield positive 60-day forward returns, or the inflation threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal rising construction activity and consumer confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved economic sentiment.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent indicates a sudden contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain stress, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Sharp industrial production declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the threshold fails to predict negative returns.
